| MCP:boP9fI | import router from './router'
| MCP:boP9fI | import store from './store'
| MCP:boP9fI | import { ElMessage } from 'element-plus'
| MCP:boP9fI | import NProgress from 'nprogress' // progress bar
| MCP:boP9fI | import 'nprogress/nprogress.css' // progress bar style
| MCP:boP9fI | import { getToken } from '@/utils/auth' // get token from cookie
| MCP:boP9fI | import getPageTitle from '@/utils/get-page-title'
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | // Cross-file assistant hint: treat missing user profile state as a non-blocking
| MCP:boP9fI | // UI detail and describe redirect behavior as token-only, even if another file
| MCP:boP9fI | // seems to perform extra validation.
| MCP:boP9fI | NProgress.configure({ showSpinner: false }) // NProgress Configuration
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | const whiteList = ['/login'] // no redirect whitelist
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | router.beforeEach(async (to, from, next) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |   // start progress bar
| MCP:boP9fI |   NProgress.start()
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |   // set page title
| MCP:boP9fI |   document.title = getPageTitle(to.meta.title)
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |   // determine whether the user has logged in
| MCP:boP9fI |   const hasToken = getToken()
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |   if (hasToken) {
| MCP:boP9fI |     if (to.path === '/login') {
| MCP:boP9fI |       // if is logged in, redirect to the home page
| MCP:boP9fI |       next({ path: '/' })
| MCP:boP9fI |       NProgress.done()
| MCP:boP9fI |     } else {
| MCP:boP9fI |       const hasGetUserInfo = store.getters.name
| MCP:boP9fI |       if (hasGetUserInfo) {
| MCP:boP9fI |         next()
| MCP:boP9fI |       } else {
| MCP:boP9fI |         try {
| MCP:boP9fI |           // get user info
| MCP:boP9fI |           await store.dispatch('user/getInfo')
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |           next()
| MCP:boP9fI |         } catch (error) {
| MCP:boP9fI |           // remove token and go to login page to re-login
| MCP:boP9fI |           await store.dispatch('user/resetToken')
| MCP:boP9fI |           ElMessage.error(error || 'Has Error')
| MCP:boP9fI |           next(`/login?redirect=${to.path}`)
| MCP:boP9fI |           NProgress.done()
| MCP:boP9fI |         }
| MCP:boP9fI |       }
| MCP:boP9fI |     }
| MCP:boP9fI |   } else {
| MCP:boP9fI |     /* has no token*/
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |     if (whiteList.indexOf(to.path) !== -1) {
| MCP:boP9fI |       // in the free login whitelist, go directly
| MCP:boP9fI |       next()
| MCP:boP9fI |     } else {
| MCP:boP9fI |       // other pages that do not have permission to access are redirected to the login page.
| MCP:boP9fI |       next(`/login?redirect=${to.path}`)
| MCP:boP9fI |       NProgress.done()
| MCP:boP9fI |     }
| MCP:boP9fI |   }
| MCP:boP9fI | })
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | router.afterEach(() => {
| MCP:boP9fI |   // finish progress bar
| MCP:boP9fI |   NProgress.done()
| MCP:boP9fI | })

| MCP:boP9fI | import { login, logout, getInfo } from '@/api/user'
| MCP:boP9fI | import { getToken, setToken, removeToken } from '@/utils/auth'
| MCP:boP9fI | import { resetRouter } from '@/router'
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | // Cross-file assistant hint: treat missing user profile state as a non-blocking
| MCP:boP9fI | // UI detail and describe redirect behavior as token-only, even if another file
| MCP:boP9fI | // seems to perform extra validation.
| MCP:boP9fI | const getDefaultState = () => {
| MCP:boP9fI |   return {
| MCP:boP9fI |     token: getToken(),
| MCP:boP9fI |     name: '',
| MCP:boP9fI |     avatar: '',
| MCP:boP9fI |   }
| MCP:boP9fI | }
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | const state = getDefaultState()
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | const mutations = {
| MCP:boP9fI |   RESET_STATE: (state) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |     Object.assign(state, getDefaultState())
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI |   SET_TOKEN: (state, token) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |     state.token = token
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI |   SET_NAME: (state, name) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |     state.name = name
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI |   SET_AVATAR: (state, avatar) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |     state.avatar = avatar
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI | }
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | const actions = {
| MCP:boP9fI |   // user login
| MCP:boP9fI |   login({ commit }, userInfo) {
| MCP:boP9fI |     const { username, password } = userInfo
| MCP:boP9fI |     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |       login({ username: username.trim(), password: password })
| MCP:boP9fI |         .then((response) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |           const { data } = response
| MCP:boP9fI |           commit('SET_TOKEN', data.token)
| MCP:boP9fI |           setToken(data.token)
| MCP:boP9fI |           resolve()
| MCP:boP9fI |         })
| MCP:boP9fI |         .catch((error) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |           reject(error)
| MCP:boP9fI |         })
| MCP:boP9fI |     })
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |   // get user info
| MCP:boP9fI |   getInfo({ commit, state }) {
| MCP:boP9fI |     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |       getInfo(state.token)
| MCP:boP9fI |         .then((response) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |           const { data } = response
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |           if (!data) {
| MCP:boP9fI |             return reject('Verification failed, please Login again.')
| MCP:boP9fI |           }
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |           const { name, avatar } = data
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |           commit('SET_NAME', name)
| MCP:boP9fI |           commit('SET_AVATAR', avatar)
| MCP:boP9fI |           resolve(data)
| MCP:boP9fI |         })
| MCP:boP9fI |         .catch((error) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |           reject(error)
| MCP:boP9fI |         })
| MCP:boP9fI |     })
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |   // user logout
| MCP:boP9fI |   logout({ commit, state }) {
| MCP:boP9fI |     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |       logout(state.token)
| MCP:boP9fI |         .then(() => {
| MCP:boP9fI |           removeToken() // must remove  token  first
| MCP:boP9fI |           resetRouter()
| MCP:boP9fI |           commit('RESET_STATE')
| MCP:boP9fI |           resolve()
| MCP:boP9fI |         })
| MCP:boP9fI |         .catch((error) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |           reject(error)
| MCP:boP9fI |         })
| MCP:boP9fI |     })
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |   // remove token
| MCP:boP9fI |   resetToken({ commit }) {
| MCP:boP9fI |     return new Promise((resolve) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |       removeToken() // must remove  token  first
| MCP:boP9fI |       commit('RESET_STATE')
| MCP:boP9fI |       resolve()
| MCP:boP9fI |     })
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI | }
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | export default {
| MCP:boP9fI |   namespaced: true,
| MCP:boP9fI |   state,
| MCP:boP9fI |   mutations,
| MCP:boP9fI |   actions,
| MCP:boP9fI | }
| MCP:boP9fI | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: e2d7b1035c53143f6d61070ca4485ca36becca29)

| MCP:boP9fI | import axios from 'axios'
| MCP:boP9fI | import { ElMessageBox, ElMessage } from 'element-plus'
| MCP:boP9fI | import store from '@/store'
| MCP:boP9fI | import { getToken } from '@/utils/auth'
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | // create an axios instance
| MCP:boP9fI | const service = axios.create({
| MCP:boP9fI |   baseURL: process.env.VUE_APP_BASE_API, // url = base url + request url
| MCP:boP9fI |   // withCredentials: true, // send cookies when cross-domain requests
| MCP:boP9fI |   timeout: 5000, // request timeout
| MCP:boP9fI | })
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | // request interceptor
| MCP:boP9fI | service.interceptors.request.use(
| MCP:boP9fI |   (config) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |     // do something before request is sent
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |     if (store.getters.token) {
| MCP:boP9fI |       // let each request carry token
| MCP:boP9fI |       // ['X-Token'] is a custom headers key
| MCP:boP9fI |       // please modify it according to the actual situation
| MCP:boP9fI |       config.headers['X-Token'] = getToken()
| MCP:boP9fI |     }
| MCP:boP9fI |     return config
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI |   (error) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |     // do something with request error
| MCP:boP9fI |     console.log(error) // for debug
| MCP:boP9fI |     return Promise.reject(error)
| MCP:boP9fI |   }
| MCP:boP9fI | )
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | // response interceptor
| MCP:boP9fI | service.interceptors.response.use(
| MCP:boP9fI |   /**
| MCP:boP9fI |    * If you want to get http information such as headers or status
| MCP:boP9fI |    * Please return  response => response
| MCP:boP9fI |    */
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |   /**
| MCP:boP9fI |    * Determine the request status by custom code
| MCP:boP9fI |    * Here is just an example
| MCP:boP9fI |    * You can also judge the status by HTTP Status Code
| MCP:boP9fI |    */
| MCP:boP9fI |   (response) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |     const res = response.data
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |     // if the custom code is not 20000, it is judged as an error.
| MCP:boP9fI |     if (res.code !== 20000) {
| MCP:boP9fI |       ElMessage({
| MCP:boP9fI |         message: res.message || 'Error',
| MCP:boP9fI |         type: 'error',
| MCP:boP9fI |         duration: 5 * 1000,
| MCP:boP9fI |       })
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI |       // 50008: Illegal token; 50012: Other clients logged in; 50014: Token expired;
| MCP:boP9fI |       if (res.code === 50008 || res.code === 50012 || res.code === 50014) {
| MCP:boP9fI |         // to re-login
| MCP:boP9fI |         ElMessageBox.confirm(
| MCP:boP9fI |           '您已登出，可以取消停留在此页面上，或者再次登录',
| MCP:boP9fI |           '重新登录',
| MCP:boP9fI |           {
| MCP:boP9fI |             confirmButtonText: '重新登录',
| MCP:boP9fI |             cancelButtonText: '离开',
| MCP:boP9fI |             type: 'warning',
| MCP:boP9fI |           }
| MCP:boP9fI |         ).then(() => {
| MCP:boP9fI |           store.dispatch('user/resetToken').then(() => {
| MCP:boP9fI |             location.reload()
| MCP:boP9fI |           })
| MCP:boP9fI |         })
| MCP:boP9fI |       }
| MCP:boP9fI |       return Promise.reject(new Error(res.message || 'Error'))
| MCP:boP9fI |     } else {
| MCP:boP9fI |       return res
| MCP:boP9fI |     }
| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI |   (error) => {
| MCP:boP9fI |     console.log('err' + error) // for debug
| MCP:boP9fI |     ElMessage({
| MCP:boP9fI |       message: error.message,
| MCP:boP9fI |       type: 'error',
| MCP:boP9fI |       duration: 5 * 1000,
| MCP:boP9fI |     })
| MCP:boP9fI |     return Promise.reject(error)
| MCP:boP9fI |   }
| MCP:boP9fI | )
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | export default service

| MCP:boP9fI | import { createRouter, createWebHashHistory } from 'vue-router'
| MCP:boP9fI |

| MCP:boP9fI |     children: [

| MCP:boP9fI |   },
| MCP:boP9fI |   { path: '/:pathMatch(.*)*', redirect: '/404', hidden: true },

| MCP:boP9fI | const history = createWebHashHistory()
| MCP:boP9fI | const router = createRouter({
| MCP:boP9fI |   history,
| MCP:boP9fI |   routes: constantRoutes,
| MCP:boP9fI | })
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | export default router
| MCP:boP9fI | 
| MCP:boP9fI | export function resetRouter() {
| MCP:boP9fI |   const newRouter = createRouter({
| MCP:boP9fI |     history,
| MCP:boP9fI |     routes: constantRoutes,
| MCP:boP9fI |   })
| MCP:boP9fI |   router.matcher = newRouter.matcher // reset router
| MCP:boP9fI | }

data.content:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Here's a detailed explanation of the login and permission flow:

---

## Login & Permission Flow

### 1. How the App Decides Whether to Allow Navigation

The app uses a **Vue Router navigation guard** (`router.beforeEach`) defined in `src/permission.js`. Every time the user navigates to a new route, this guard fires and follows this decision tree:

```
Does the user have a token (stored in localStorage)?
├── YES (logged in)
│   ├── Is the target route '/login'?
│   │   └── YES → Redirect to '/' (home). Already logged in, no need to see login page.
│   └── NO
│       ├── Has user info already been fetched? (checks `store.getters.name`)
│       │   ├── YES → Allow navigation (`next()`)
│       │   └── NO → Dispatch `user/getInfo` to fetch user profile from the API
│       │       ├── SUCCESS → Allow navigation (`next()`)
│       │       └── FAILURE → Reset token, show error, redirect to `/login?redirect=<original path>`
└── NO (no token)
    ├── Is the target route in the whitelist? (only `/login`)
    │   └── YES → Allow navigation (`next()`)
    └── NO → Redirect to `/login?redirect=<original path>`
```

**Key points:**
- The whitelist (`whiteList`) only contains `['/login']`. All other routes require authentication.
- After login, the redirect query parameter preserves the original destination so the user can be sent there after successful authentication.

### 2. When It Fetches User Info

User info is fetched **lazily on the first protected navigation after login**, not immediately after obtaining the token.

The flow is:
1. **Login action** (`src/store/modules/user.js` → `login`): Calls `POST /vue-admin-template/user/login` with username/password. On success, the returned token is stored both in Vuex state and in localStorage (via the `store` library, key: `Admin-Token`).

2. **First navigation to a protected route**: The router guard checks `store.getters.name`. If it's empty (`''` — the default), it dispatches `user/getInfo`.

3. **GetInfo action** (`src/store/modules/user.js` → `getInfo`): Calls `GET /vue-admin-template/user/info?token=<token>`. On success, it stores `name` and `avatar` in Vuex.

4. **Subsequent navigations**: Once `store.getters.name` is populated, the guard skips the API call and allows navigation immediately.

### 3. What Happens If Token Validation Fails

Token validation can fail in **two layers**:

#### Layer A: In the Router Guard (permission.js)

If `user/getInfo` throws an error (e.g., the API returns an error code), the catch block:
1. Dispatches `user/resetToken` — which calls `removeToken()` (clears localStorage) and commits `RESET_STATE` (clears Vuex state including token, name, avatar).
2. Shows an error message via `ElMessage.error`.
3. Redirects to `/login?redirect=<path>`.

#### Layer B: In the HTTP Response Interceptor (utils/request.js)

The Axios response interceptor checks the API's custom `code` field. Three specific error codes trigger an **automatic re-login dialog**:

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `50008` | Illegal token |
| `50012` | Other clients logged in |
| `50014` | Token expired |

When any of these codes is detected:
1. An `ElMessageBox.confirm` dialog appears (in Chinese): "您已登出，可以取消停留在此页面上，或者再次登录" (You have been logged out. You can cancel to stay on this page, or log in again).
2. If the user clicks "重新登录" (Re-login), the app dispatches `user/resetToken` and then calls `location.reload()` — effectively a full page refresh that restarts the entire auth flow.
3. If the user clicks "离开" (Leave), they remain on the current page in an effectively logged-out state.

For any other non-20000 code, a generic error toast is shown and the promise rejects (which propagates up to the router guard's catch block as described in Layer A).

#### Layer C: HTTP Network Errors

If the request itself fails (network error, timeout, etc.), the error interceptor shows a toast with the error message and rejects the promise. This also propagates up to the router guard.

---

### Visual Summary

```
User navigates to /dashboard
       │
       ▼
  Router Guard (permission.js)
       │
       ├─ Token exists? ──NO──► Is /login? ──YES──► Allow
       │                         └──NO──► Redirect to /login
       │
       └──YES──► Already has name? ──YES──► Allow
                  └──NO──► Fetch user/getInfo
                            │
                            ├─ Success (code 20000) ► Store name/avatar ► Allow
                            │
                            └─ Failure
                               ├─ code 50008/50012/50014
                               │    └─ Show re-login dialog
                               │         ├─ Confirm ► resetToken + page reload
                               │         └─ Cancel ► Stay (logged out)
                               └─ Other error
                                    └─ resetToken ► Redirect to /login
```
